description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped stones, each with a rugged, natural appearance and a dark gray coloration. The surfaces are uneven, featuring a mix of smooth and jagged areas, with some subtle glossy highlights suggesting slight moisture or polished spots. The largest stone is broad and somewhat flat, while the two smaller stones are more rounded and compact, positioned adjacent to the main stone. The texture is predominantly rough, with visible creases, indentations, and natural weathering marks. There are no visible artificial carvings or patterns, and the stones appear solid and heavy. This grouping would be typical as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park, sidewalk edge, or landscaped area.
description: A cluster of three dark gray, irregularly shaped stones with rough, natural surfaces and subtle glossy highlights. The largest stone is broad and flat, accompanied by two smaller, rounded stones.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the largest stone in the foreground with its broad, flat surface and two smaller stones to the right, highlighting their size difference and surface texture.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the stones from the opposite side, emphasizing the thickness of the largest stone and the rounded shapes of the smaller stones in front.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the stones from a side angle, with the two smaller stones more prominent and the largest stone's elongated shape visible.
description_view_3: View 3 focuses on the back of the largest stone, showing its broad, slightly sloped surface and the smaller stones partially obscured behind it.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with other stones on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and natural creases with no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: Slightly polished with some rough areas, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, grass, or other landscaping elements, providing a subtle contrast to paved or built environments
